# Code statistics per variant

<!-- Generated by scripts/code-stats (the .sh / .ps1 pair, markdown format). Do not edit by hand; rerun the script. -->

One product, the same contract, implemented across many stacks. Line counts
come from [`tokei`](https://github.com/XAMPPRocky/tokei) and are split by role
so stacks compare on equal footing:

| Role | What it counts |
|---|---|
| **app** | Implementation source and runtime/behavioural config (`application.yml`, `nginx.conf`, `routes`, `*.sql` migrations, `server.xml`, ...) |
| **tests** | Test source (under `test/ tests/ __tests__/ spec/`, or named `*.test.*`, `*.spec.*`, `*Test.*`, `*Spec.*`, `*_test.go`, `test_*.py`) |
| **infra** | Build, dependency, tooling and container config (`build.gradle`, `pom.xml`, `*.csproj`, `Cargo.toml`, `go.mod`, `package.json`, `Dockerfile`, ...) |
| **docs** | Prose (`*.md`, `LICENSE`, ...) |

`tokei` respects `.gitignore` and skips hidden files, so build output and
vendored caches are excluded. Raw LOC is a shape/verbosity signal, not a measure
of effort or quality. Rust `#[cfg(test)]` tests live in the file they test and
cannot be split by path, so they count as **app** for `rust-axum`.

**App / Tests / Infra** are tokei `code` lines (standard LOC). **Docs** is prose
lines (`code + comments`) — Markdown has no `code` lines, so a code-only figure
would read 0. `Test%` is `tests / (app + tests)` code. Regenerate with
`scripts/code-stats.sh --format markdown --out docs/CODE-STATS.md` or
`scripts/code-stats.ps1 -Format markdown -Out docs/CODE-STATS.md`.
